zh.Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛
zh.Cowthorpe, North Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾考福索普
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de Burgh於1405年出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾考福索普時，他的父親Sir Hugh de Burgh26歲，母親Elizabeth de la Pole20歲。他於1429年與Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl在蘇格蘭培爾阿爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1487年在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年82歲。

zh-hans.Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England: 英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛
zh-hans.Cowthorpe, North Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾考福索普
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas de Burgh于1405年出生在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾考福索普时，他的父亲Sir Hugh de Burgh26岁，母亲Elizabeth de la Pole20岁。他于1429年与Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl在苏格兰培爾阿爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1487年在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de Burgh was born in 1405, in Cowthorpe, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Hugh de Burgh, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth de la Pole, was 20. He married Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl in 1429, in Atholl, Perthshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1487, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de Burgh est né en 1405, à Cowthorpe, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Hugh de Burgh, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de la Pole, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl en 1429, à Atholl, Perthshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1487, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Burghが1405年にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアコウソルペで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh de Burghは26歳，母Elizabeth de la Poleは20歳であった。1429年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアアソルでElizabeth Percy Lady of Athollと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1487年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Burgh(이)가 1405년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 코우소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Hugh de Burgh(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de la Pole(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1429년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 앳홀에서 Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1487년 영국 링코실르 게인스버루그h에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de Burgh родился в 1405 г. в местности коутаурп, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Hugh de Burgh было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de la Pole, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl в 1429 г. в местности атолл, пиртшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1487 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет.
